observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Administrator and the MGSO to hasten the processing up to
finalization/operationalization of the proposed new composition of the Inventory
Committee;

b. Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to systematically update and maintain all
required subsidiary records such as: PPE Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger
Cards and Property Cards; and

c. MGSO and all the members of the Inventory Committee to prepare the
Preliminary Activities, including the preparation of the Physical Inventory Plan
and conduct a full and comprehensive inventory taking of all the Municipality’s
PPEs, among others, following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for
 the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts so as to ensure that all PPE
balances recorded in the books are verifiable in terms of their existence,
condition, and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
reevaluate the composition of the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special
Education Tax Receivable, including the Deferred RPT and Deferred SET, and adjust
accordingly to report the accurate accounts’ balances for CY 2024. Exclude
penalties from uncollected tax revenues as this shall be recognized as income on
the year it would be collected, pursuant to the provisions of the New Government
Accounting System Manual, so as to present an accurate and reliable balances of
the affected accounts in the financial statements of the Municipal Government at
year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
conduct a thorough verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of all
dormant accounts or those accounts that have been non-moving for ten years or
more, and adopt the procedures for the proper disposition of dormant accounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 to achieve a fairly presented accounts in
the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Social Worker and Development Officer,
Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Engineer adhere to Rule IV of the 2009
Revised Rules of Procedure of the Commission on Audit on the processing of the
request for write-off of accounts, and the request for relief from accountability,
where applicable, so as to reflect the correct balance and valuation of the affected
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. HRMO to trace back and reconstruct, if possible, the historical payroll records,
including the remittances data to the extent possible, and maintain a systematic
and updated personnel database;

b. Municipal Accountant to immediately start the preparation and maintenance of
individual subsidiary ledgers for all statutory deductions and corresponding
remittances to facilitate ease in reconciliation of records and provide readily
available information as of a given period;

c. HRMO to adopt an appropriate and effective monitoring tool for the timely
remittance of amounts due to the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG within the
prescribed period and to prevent and detect over/under remittance of amount
withheld and deducted from employees; and

d. HRMO to cause the immediate registration of employees without official records
with Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth and remittance of withheld contributions from
them so as to ensure full identification to whom such remittances shall be
accounted for.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to fast-track the implementation
of the 11 development projects that have not been completed and implemented as
at year-end so as to attain the full benefits accorded to each of these projects.

We also recommended that the Municipal Development Council, during the
planning stage, to assess and plan thoroughly the necessity and appropriateness
of the projects to be included and funded by the 20% Development, so as to prevent
frequent realignment of projects, particularly nearing the close of the year, and to
focus on the full utilization of funds for priority projects of the 20%DF, and ensure
that only those that are procurement and implementation ready will be prioritized
pursuant to DBM-DOF- DILG JMC No. 2020-01 for effective management of the
Fund.

Further, we recommended that the Local Finance Committee to cause the
immediate reversion of savings from completed projects to unappropriated surplus
of the General Fund in order to finance other developmental programs, projects and
activities that are truly beneficial to its constituents, thus attain the purpose of
20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Finance Committee to
cause the immediate reversion of the unutilized Special Trust Funds into the
General Fund, as it had exceeded the prescribed five-year retention period,
including of the savings from completed projects to unappropriated surplus of the
General Fund to finance other social services and other disaster risk reduction and
management activities of the Municipality that are truly beneficial to its
constituents, pursuant to Section 21 of the RA No. 10121 and Section 5.1.13 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare a duly approved Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties and Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Semi-Expendable Property as basis of the disposal
proceedings and submit these reports within the prescribed period; and

b. Disposal Committee to immediately prioritize the disposal of unserviceable
properties in accordance with COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 to avoid
further deterioration, leading to a lesser resale value, and unclog occupied
unnecessary spaces in the premises of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Require the GFPS Executive Committee to ensure the timely submission of the
GPB, GAD AR and other required GAD-related reports to the DILG for the
effective and efficient implementation of the identified GAD activities, and the
judicious utilization of the GAD budget in accordance with the provisions of
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended by
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01;

b. Reconstitute a functional GAD Focal Point System, whereby the members
composing the Executive Committee, Technical Working Group and/or a
Secretariat are all efficient and effective in the discharge of their respective roles
and responsibilities;

c. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, with the
assistance of the GFPS, to strictly spearhead the setting up and maintenance of
the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,
programming and policy formulation; and

d. Require the GFPS Technical Working Group and/or a Secretariat to observe the
guidelines, procedures and timelines on GPB and GAD AR preparation,
consolidation and submission.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource and
Management Officer to strictly adopt the required monthly net take-home pay of
employees of not lower than P5,000.00 pursuant to the directive embodied in the
2024 GAA so as to preserve the employees’ morale and work performance. If the
total authorized deductions shall reduce net take-home pay to less than P5,000.00,
deductions should be made in accordance with the order of preference under
Section 56 of 2024 GAA.

We also recommended that the Municipal Cooperative Development Office exercise
due diligence and caution in granting loan applications to its members, depending on
their financial and paying capacity. Refrain from approving the loan application of a
member who could not meet the required monthly net take-home pay, following
existing laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that the Local School Board submit the Quarterly and Annual
SEF Utilization Reports for CY 2024, and in succeeding years, to the DepEd Central
Office, through appropriate channels, and furnish copies thereof to the
Sangguniang Bayan and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG; and strictly comply
on the submission of copies of the SEF Budgets to concerned Municipal Offices in
accordance with Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017 to ensure
transparency and accountability in reporting the allocation and utilization of the
Special Education Fund to the intended users of information.

We recommended and the Local Chief executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant:
a.	To undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the above payable accounts and regularly prepare Schedule of Accounts Payable and Other Payables to keep track of the list of all the outstanding payable, creditor/suppliers, and how much is owed to substantiate the accuracy of the amount of claim against the Municipality;
b.	Prepare and maintain complete subsidiary ledger and schedule/aging of Accounts Payable and ensure that the schedule is reconciled with the balance reflected on the General Ledger and the financial statement; and
c.	Undertake a thorough review and analysis of the dormant payables to identify outstanding invalid claims, administrative or judicial, has been filed or that are not covered by perfected contracts and make a proper presentation for its possible reversion to the General.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant presents loans payable that are due to be settled within twelve months after the reporting date be reported in the current liability portion in the statement of financial position and make proper disclosure and information of all loans payable in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct:
a. 	The Municipal Accountant to investigate the dormant and unidentified balances of the Due to NGAs account to determine its nature, specific purpose and the source agency from which the funds were received. Thereafter, facilitate evaluation to determine whether the corresponding projects have been implemented and return the unused funds to the source agency, if warranted; and
b. 	The Municipal Treasurer to return to the Source Agency any unutilized funds which are no longer needed after the completion of the specific programs and projects pursuant to Item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Services Officer towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of all Inventory Accounts to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials; and
The Municipal Accountant and designated General Services Officer maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of Inventory, keep track the actual Supplies Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant, for ensuing transactions refrain the recording of Food Supplies for Distribution/Expense for cost of goods issued/distributed to people affected by calamities/disasters/ground conflicts instead, record to Welfare Goods for distribution and Welfare Goods Expenses upon distribution.

We recommended and the Management agreed that for ensuing transactions, the Municipal Accountant record all Semi-Expendable Properties issued to end-user by debiting relative Expense Account and credit Semi-Expendable Properties as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024. Likewise, make necessary adjusting entries by debiting prior period adjustment and crediting Semi-Expendable Properties

We recommended and the Management agreed that the designated General Service Officer and/or BAC Secretariat to submit copy of all contracts, purchase orders and corresponding documents forming part thereof to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from the date of perfection and as well as assigned personnel in-charge of its preparation and submission as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Adhere with Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 and the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. dated June 10, 2016 on the maintenance of special accounts for Municipality’s Local Economic Enterprise; and
The Municipal Accountant maintain separate book of accounts as well as prepared the financial statements for each of the economic enterprise in accordance with Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to provide Management with the relevant financial information needed for decision and policy making.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2024 on the required analysis of dormant accounts and to comply with all the conditions and requirements of the said circular for the possible request for the write off and the proper disposal thereafter of the specified dormant receivables from the Commission on Audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to fully utilize developmental PPAs funded under 20% DF. See to it that during the preparation of the Annual Budget for 20% Development Fund includes only PPAs which are feasible and procurement-and-implementation ready that could be accomplished within the specific timeline for completion and utilized within the year.
We further recommended and the Management agreed that at the end of each year, review all unimplemented projects and verify whether they can be implemented in the succeeding year. Otherwise, cause the reversion or re-alignment of the appropriated funds including those savings from previous completed PPAs so that same may be used effectively and efficiently for more appropriate priority development projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Exert effort in implementing the PPAs identified in its LDRRMFIP in order to increase its capabilities and preparedness to respond in case of disaster or emergencies; and
All savings from completed projects be reprogrammed and unprogrammed appropriation in the Special Trust Fund be appropriated for disaster related activities and be included in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned Municipal Officials exert concerted efforts to intensify collections Real Property Tax including the interest thereon so that more development programs may be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended and the Management agreed to maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to require the GAD Focal Point System to prepare and complete the Municipality’s GAD Agenda 2022-2025 following the steps provided under the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Discontinued the procurement of wreaths using public funds, unless such expenses are:
i. 	Expressly authorized by law or has specific guidelines;
ii. 	Directly related to official government functions or mandates; and
iii. 	Supported by proper documentation and justification.
Review and strengthen internal controls on the approval and processing of expenditures to ensure strict compliance with government accounting and auditing rules, particularly the principles of necessity, legality, and public purpose.

In response to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, create an Inventory Committee for the sole purpose of conducting a one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s PPE which shall be composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory.

We recommended and Municipal Accountant agreed to:

Make the necessary adjustments in the books to correct all erroneous recording/classifications noted by debiting appropriate PPE accounts and crediting prior period adjustments; and

Check the nature and source documents for each type of transactions should be analyzed and reviewed before recording in the books of accounts to maintain reliability, accuracy, and fairness of presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and Designate GSO agreed to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials which shall be reported in the RPCI and submitted to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure optimum utilization of the calamity fund for expenditures that address the key objectives of disaster risk reduction and management as envisioned under R.A. No. 10121.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:

Require the MPDO and Municipal Engineer to properly plan the programs/projects by addressing first issues related to its implementation and if readily implementable the within prescribed timeline to avoid such delay in the implementation in the future; 

Require the MPDO and Municipal Engineer to have a close-in monitoring and supervision on the project and provide accurate report. As a case maybe, impose upon to the contractor in default the liquidated damages an amount to at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay from the contract duration expires, including any time extension duly granted; and

Cause the immediate completion of the Project.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the pre-evaluation report with sufficient details, the scope of work and secure a copy of documents indicating the history of repair before the conduct of pre-repair inspection report of the equipment and vehicle subject for repair; 

Require the preparation of Waste Material Reports of the damaged replaced part and have is inspected before disposal is made; and

The Municipal Accountant cautiously check the completeness of all the pertinent supporting documents before allowing the payment.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

Use the prescribed forms, require all drivers to prepare a Driver’s Trip Tickets with all information required properly supplied and prepare and submit a Monthly Report of Official Travel for each Vehicle; and

Prepare a monthly fuel consumption report and submit copy to the auditor within the first 10 days of the following month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), Municipal Treasurer and other concerned Municipal Officials exert concerted efforts to intensify collections of Real Property Tax including the interest thereon so that more development programs may be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MGSO-Designate to expedite the retrieval of other pertinent documents for donated properties and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the copy for recording in the books of accounts and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements;
b. MGSO-Designate to facilitate the registration and transfer of ownership in the name of the Municipality of donated Transportation Equipment; and
c. For future donation-in-kind, we recommended that the MGSO-Designate secure all the necessary documents/records from the Donor to prevent recurring issues as in the foregoing.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Instruct the MGSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to perform the preliminary activities outlined in the Circular before the conduct of inventory taking. Start by obtaining and comparing the Property and Accounting records using the latest Report on the RPCPPE/Inventory Report and PPE Ledger Cards and prepare a list of PPE items that are unreconciled. These documents shall be considered as inventory working papers and shall be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in preparing the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP);
b. Ensure that the Inventory Committee is composed of adequate members who will devote themselves to the conduct of inventory taking in accordance with the approved PIP; and
c. Immediately start the application of one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjustments to properly reflect in the books the unrecorded asset accounts, including corresponding accumulated depreciation and submit the same to the Audit Team; and
b. Recognize appropriate asset, revenue and expense accounts in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of funds received from other agencies arising from non-exchange transactions and cause the preparation and submission of the Statement of Financial Performance of the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment/reclassification for each item with cost below the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 or those considered as Semi-expendable property following COA Circular No. 2022-004 to reflect fairly the accounts balances in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for property insurance of the Municipality subject to its financial capacity; and
b. Municipal General Services Officer-Designate to prepare the Property Inventory Form and cause the appraisal of the properties and submit them for application of insurance with GSIS to protect the Management thru indemnification in cases of damage or losses due to fortuitous events particularly the imminent devastation of the Taal Volcano eruption.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resources and Management Officer (HRMO) to verify the veracity of Due to GSIS, Due to PAGIBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts balances. If necessary, make representation to the inter-agencies to facilitate reconciliation of records and adjustment of any disparity.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforesaid GSIS, PAGIBIG and Philhealth regulations on withholding of contributions and remittance/payment of withheld amounts and/or loan payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer to prepare a timetable for the implementation of the remaining unimplemented projects funded out of the 20% DF and submit to the COA Audit Team; and
b. MPDC to evaluate the technical viability of development projects at the planning stage to ensure that only development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready will be funded by the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all contracts for infrastructure projects are supported with DOLE-approved; and
b. BAC requires the interested bidders to include in their bids the cost of CHSP as well as the protective equipment to be provided to their laborers.
We also recommended strict compliance with DOLE DO No. 13, s. 1998 and Section 37.2.3(f) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, require the HRMO-Designate to :
a. Come up with concrete policy guidelines relative to the hiring and rehiring of JO personnel to establish a clear expectation and protect the rights of both the employees and the Municipality; and
b. Provide equivalent designation for each hired and rehired JO and set a standardized wage rate based on such designation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive Committee to require the GAD Focal Point System and the Technical Working Group to:
a. Ensure that at least 5% of the total LGU’s Annual Budget is set aside as GAD Budget for the implementation of activities supporting GAD PPAs; and
b. Attend an extensive training program on how to administer the HGDG to assure the proper determination of the PPAs that may be attributed to the GAD budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. GAD Focal Person to immediately act on the comments and recommendations of the DILG in the GAD-PBMS via DILG Intranet pertaining to the deficiencies noted in the GPB and be mindful thereof to avoid delay and lapses in the preparation of same report in succeeding years;
b. GFPS, specifically the GFPS-Technical Working Group, to equip themselves with the necessary skills and knowledge and deepen their understanding of the facets of GAD planning, budgeting, implementation, monitoring and evaluation; and
c. GFPS-Technical Working Group and GAD Focal Person to strictly comply on the submission of the GPB and GAD AR within the prescribed period to facilitate timely evaluation and validation of GAD reports, budget, and utilization.

Make the necessary adjustments in the books to correct all erroneous recording/classifications noted by debiting appropriate PPE accounts and crediting prior period adjustments; and
Check the nature and source documents for each type of transactions should be analyzed and reviewed before recording in the books of accounts to maintain reliability, accuracy, and fairness of presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and Designate GSO agreed to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials which shall be reported in the RPCI and submitted to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

Their coordination towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of all Inventory Accounts to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials; and
Maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of Inventory, keep track the actual Supplies Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure optimum utilization of the calamity fund for expenditures that address the key objectives of disaster risk reduction and management as envisioned under R.A. No. 10121.

We recommended and Management agreed to fully utilize developmental PPAs funded under the 20% DF. See to it that during the preparation of the Annual Budget for 20% Development Fund include only PPAs which are feasible and procurement-and-implementation ready that could be accomplished within the specific timeline for completion.

Use the prescribed forms, require all drivers to prepare a Driver’s Trip Tickets with all information required properly supplied and prepare and submit a Monthly Report of Official Travel for each Vehicle; and
Prepare a monthly fuel consumption report and submit copy to the auditor within the first 10 days of the following month.

We recommended and both the designated General Service Officer/BAC Secretariat agreed to submit copy of all contracts, purchase orders and corresponding documents forming part thereof to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from the date of perfection and as well as assigned personnel in-charge of its preparation and submission as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned departments that for all ensuing transactions, they should prepare activity/budget design/proposal before the implementation of the activity.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately demand liquidation reports with invoices, receipts and other supporting documents of the financial assistance made for CY 2022 and for all ensuing transactions to the Balete PNP in compliance to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 for proper accounting and recording.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and Secretariat to complied strictly in the posting requirement of awarded contracts to PhilGEPS pursuant to the provisions of the RA 9184 and its Updated IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe proper journal entries for recording of trust receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund and to prepare and submit the Statement of Financial Performance of the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE annually and submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year;

The inventory taking shall be per PPE account/each category of assets and prepare the RPCPPE on the result thereof in conformity with the above-mentioned Manual for LGUs.

The accounting office and designated General Services Officer religiously maintain the PPELC and Stock Cards/Property Cards; and

The Accounting office and General Services Office reconcile PPE accounts between PPELC and Property Cards.

We recommended and management agreed that:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE annually and submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year;

The inventory taking shall be per PPE account/each category of assets and prepare the RPCPPE on the result thereof in conformity with the above-mentioned Manual for LGUs.

The accounting office and designated General Services Officer religiously maintain the PPELC and Stock Cards/Property Cards; and

The Accounting office and General Services Office reconcile PPE accounts between PPELC and Property Cards.

We recommended and management agreed that:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE annually and submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year;

The inventory taking shall be per PPE account/each category of assets and prepare the RPCPPE on the result thereof in conformity with the above-mentioned Manual for LGUs.

The accounting office and designated General Services Officer religiously maintain the PPELC and Stock Cards/Property Cards; and

The Accounting office and General Services Office reconcile PPE accounts between PPELC and Property Cards.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant the following:
a) Properly provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; and

b)	Comply with all the conditions and requirements for possible filing for request of authority to write off dormant receivable accounts by the Local Chief Executive to the Commission on Audit.

Foregoing observations considered, we recommended that Management updates the
composition of the Inventory Committee with an adequate number of members.
We also reiterate our audit recommendations in prior years that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to: 1) conduct the complete physical inventory of all the PPEs
of the Municipality, investigate or cause the disposition of those properties reported
in lumpsum amounts, and 2) prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count
of Property, Plant and Equipment, in conformity with the format and contents
prescribed by Annex 49 Chapter II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Ensure
the timely submission of the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate
reconciliation of records and the Audit Team for verification. Failure to do so shall
automatically cause the suspension of payment of salaries of the officials
concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.
b. Municipal General Services Officer Designate to prepare and submit to the Audit
Team the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), setting forth the target activities in the
validation and implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; and
c. concerned offices to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 375 RA No. 7160
and COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer the actual cost of the works accomplished for
the three infrastructure projects ongoing construction from Other Land Improvements
and Markets accounts to the CIP using the appropriate asset classification, and to
revert the amount deducted from the Due to NGAs account. Afterward, draw a JEV to
reclassify the total amount of financial assistance received from PAGCOR from Due to
NGAs to Due to GOCCs.
We also recommended strictly compliance with pertinent provisions of Section 50 of
the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1 and Paragraph 27 of IPSASs 1.

Prospectively, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Schedule of Unreleased Checks annually and
submit it to the Municipal Accountant in time for the closing of the books; and
b. Municipal Accountant to draw the adjusting entry to restore the Cash in the Bank
and appropriate liability accounts equivalent to the number of unreleased checks
at the end of the year.

For substantial and transparent financial reporting, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate information for
accounts with materials balances and significant changes in the financial statements
and fully observe the disclosure requirements of IPSAS and the use of the proforma
Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer- Designate and members of the Inventory
Committee to account for all the properties of the Municipality which were found
unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed, prepare the Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and initiate immediate
disposal proceedings to avoid further deterioration and/or wastage and
unnecessary consumption of storage space;
b. MGSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to update the property cards and
ledger cards of the duly identified unserviceable properties under their custody
and facilitate reconciliation of records;
c. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements relative to the particulars and carrying amount of the unserviceable
properties and those which were fully depreciated but are still in use by the
Municipality to achieve fair presentation of PPE balances in the financial
statements; and
d. concerned Municipal officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 379 of
RA No. 7160, paragraph 6.3.1(h) of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and paragraph 27 of
IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. draw journal entries to establish the correct balances of the Net Asset/Equity and
affected asset accounts; and
b. observe the proper journal entries in recording trust receipts and disbursements
under the Trust Fund and in closing the same upon fulfillment of the purpose or
condition for which these were granted/transferred. Refrain from making shortcut
entries for proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert all
effort and strategize ways in order to substantiate the recurring balance of the Due to
Other Funds in the amount of P1,069,668.57 and reconcile the same with its reciprocal
account Due from Other Funds pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal
officials to prioritize the implementation of the project for the provision of a suitable
and adequate size storage facility with installed racks that can accommodate all the
financial reports and other related documents of the Municipality and its 13 barangays
considering the ten-year preservation period of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. BAC Secretariat, Municipal General Services Officer, and the Municipal Accountant
to submit the lacking supporting documents of the DVs to the Audit Team and
provide justification on other noted inconsistencies and deficiencies;
b. Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat to ensure that all subsequent
procurement projects are supported with complete documentation and properly
accomplished and signed documents in compliance with the existing laws, rules
and regulations;
c. Concerned department heads, thru the Municipal Accountant to submit the proof
of distribution/utilization of the items/goods which lacked the appropriate
distribution lists for evaluation of the Team; and
d. BAC Secretariat to ensure compliance with the posting requirements for Request
for Quotation, Notice of Award and Purchase Order under the alternative mode of
procurement.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the
Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee to:
a. provide justification in writing for the issues noted in Overlapping Key Personnel
and Non-Posting of Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Contract in PhilGEPS
and submit the lacking documentary requirements enumerated in Item E;
b. meticulously conduct bid evaluation and post-qualification such that no award
shall be made to contractors with the deficiencies/inconsistencies in their bids;
and
c. ensure that documents related to the procurement transaction are properly
accomplished and signed including the dates to facilitate verification of the
veracity of the documents.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to verify the completeness of supporting documents before
processing payment claims and recording the transactions in the books of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Council to assess the viability of the
identified PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that only those projects which are
procurement and implementation ready will be given budget allocation to avoid
realignment and sudden change of projects intended for current year’s
implementation; and
b. MPDC and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records on the utilization of the
20% Development Fund to ensure transparency and reliability of financial reports.

As our recommendation, we reiterate that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) and Local
Development Council (LDC) to assess the viability of the identified PPAs in the
planning stage to ensure implementation so that local constituents and the
community would benefit at the earliest;
b. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to prepare and submit to
the local sanggunian through the LDRRMC and LDC the annual LDRRMF
Investment Plan using the prescribed format of COA Circular No. 2012-002,
specifying in detail the list of programs, activities and projects which include under
60
a separate caption those projects and programs charged against the continuing
appropriations as well as unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and implement
such projects within the timeframe set;
c. MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM
Funds to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
d. concerned Municipal officials to strictly comply with the provisions of RA No.
10121, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the Management agreed:


   1. To update or recreate Inventory  Committee  and  mobilized  additional
      competent personnel from other departments as members  and  completely
      conduct physical count of Inventory of the Municipality’s PPE annually
      and submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly accomplished RPCPPE;


   2. Instruct that inventory taking shall be per PPE account/each  category
      of assets and prepare the RPCPPE on the result thereof  in  conformity
      with the above-mentioned Manual for LGUs;

   3. To direct the Municipal Accountant  and  designated  General  Services
      Officer to religiously maintain the  PPELC  and  Stock  Cards/Property
      Cards;

   4. To direct the Municipal Accountant  and  designated  General  Services
      Officer  to  reconcile  and  analyzed  the  difference  between  their
      respective and effect necessary adjustments; and



   5. Instruct the  General  Services  Office  to  transfer  all  identified
      unserviceable properties in the Inventory  and  Inspection  Report  of
      Unserviceable Property (IIRUO) Form as prescribed in Annex 51,  Volume
      2 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, for proper disposal and basis to
      derecognize from  the  book  of  accounts,  then  undertake  necessary
      actions for disposal in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.

We recommended and  the  Local  Chief  Executive  agreed  to  direct  the
   Municipal Accountant to  strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of  COA
   Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the required analysis of
   dormant accounts and to comply with all the conditions  and  requirements
   of the said Circular for the possible request for the write  off  of  the
   specified dormant receivables from the Commission on Audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to  ensure  that  PPAs  for  the
   counterpart fund to Kalahi-CIDDS be utilized only to  contribute  to  the
   attainment  of  desirable  socio-economic  targets   and   outcomes   and
   immediately stop the charging of the operating expenses to the 20% DF  in
   accordance with Section 3.0 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated  November
   20, 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed to immediately stop the  payment
   of all wages charged against the LDRRMF and  to  exert  prudence  in  the
   disbursement of LDRRMF, see to it that  only  expenditures  appropriately
   chargeable against the said funds are those in pursuant to the provisions
   of Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and  DILG  Joint  Circular  No.  2013-01  and
   Section 4.3 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the  Bids
   and Awards Committee  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  that  for  ensuing
   relative procurement transactions, they should be strict in requiring all
   the caterers/suppliers/contractors who would like  to  transact  business
   with the  Municipal  Government  to  submit  their  current  and  updated
   Certificate of PhilGEPS Registration or the PhilGEPS Registration  Number
   to conform to the requirements of Updated IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  the  immediate
   transfer of the unexpended balance of  the  QRF  and  DRRMF-MOOE  of  the
   current year to Special Trust Fund for DRRM.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a  thorough
   analysis  of  the  unreconciled  beginning  balances  and  reconcile  the
   accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to determine whether
   all transfers of funds are authorized and received by other funds and  to
   ensure accuracy of its balances and  be  able  to  effect  the  necessary
   adjustment if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.

We recommended and the designated General Service Officer/BAC Secretariat
   agreed to submit copy of all contracts and purchase orders to the  Office
   of the Auditor within five working days from the date perfection and each
   of all the  documents  forming  part  thereof  as  prescribed  under  COA
   Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended and the BAC Chairman and Secretariat  agreed  to  complied
   strictly in the posting requirement  of  awarded  contracts  to  PhilGEPS
   pursuant to the provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to consistently verify and review all the transactions and accounts balances before closing and preparing the year-end financial statements; and b. Municipal Accountant to incorporate in the notes to financial statements the necessary information pertaining to the Municipality’s financial position and operation following the full disclosure principle.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management informed the Audit Team that corrected the error on January 2021.
observations.reason: The required information was still not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to consult with the service provider the disparity between the consolidated SL balances of all the accounts comprising Cash in Bank, Local Currency Current Account and its GL ending balances generated by ECPAC FMIS as well as other reports. For accuracy of record, we also recommended the Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the general ledger to sub-ledger balances of accounts, specifically those involving cash.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management informed the Audit Team that consultation of the concerns with the service provider has been undertaken, and other audit recommendation will be prioritized for immediate action by the accounting office.
observations.reason: No further details provided; reconciliation and other corrective actions to be prioritized and undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to discontinue granting additional cash advance to the Head of the Procuring Entity and other elected municipal officials for purposes other than official travelling expense. Ensure that no additional cash advance shall be granted to permanent appointed officials or designated disbursing officers with outstanding cash advances. Ensure liquidation is consistently submitted and recorded.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management explained that during pre-pandemic time, the concerned Municipal personnel is actually observing the proper granting of cash advances. Nevertheless, due to pandemic, they prioritized immediate assistance to frontlines and constituents, and promised compliance to the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant, Social Welfare and Development Officer, Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Engineer to exert utmost efforts in the determination of the whereabouts and retrieval of prior years’ records of accounts affiliated with the asset accounts. b. Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to persons identified with payable records. Ensure any action undertaken is adequately documented and inform COA; c. Municipal Accountant to incorporate appropriate adjusting entries where applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials submitted certifications and letters indicating difficulties: the Municipal Agriculturist certified absence of breeding stocks due to Taal eruption; the Municipal Engineer stated ongoing infrastructure projects subject of observation were not existing and she had no knowledge of the whereabouts of the subject construction in progress. The Municipal Accountant submitted a request for write-off but it was returned due to inadequate supporting documents. No comprehensive remedy yet; actions to verify and document being undertaken.
observations.reason: Certifications and letters were submitted but lacked sufficient documentary evidence to fully substantiate or adjust the dormant balances; request for write-off returned due to inadequate supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal General Services Officer: upon purchase of supplies and materials secure copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report, Purchase Order and delivery receipts; b. Municipal General Services Officer support all consumption issuances with duly filled Requisition and Issue Slip; c. Municipal Accountant to record supplies and materials using appropriate inventory accounts; d. using monthly SSMI and RIS to recognize corresponding expense; e. conduct physical count at least once a year and incorporate results in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories using the prescribed format; f. Municipal Accountant to incorporate full disclosure of inventories in notes to financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Officer-Designate commented adoption of perpetual inventory system requires dedicated time and effort; from July 2021 they started securing AIR, PO and PR upon purchase of materials. The Municipal Accountant and MGSO promised to comply and start verifying, but no full adoption yet. Some purchases recorded; physical counts ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and time; adoption of perpetual inventory system is ongoing and requires sustained effort; not yet fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical inventory of the entire properties owned by the Municipality for all funds; b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Office to reconcile their records and verify existence of unaccounted properties; c. maintain subsidiary ledger cards and property cards for each type of PPE account to facilitate checks and balances; d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and disclose required property ledger cards in notes to financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Office and Municipal Accountant have been undertaking inventory activities; Municipal Engineer and others submitted partial updated reports on local road networks; efforts to reconcile and prepare property cards are ongoing. Some submissions made; however full reconciliation and documentation remain incomplete.
observations.reason: Incomplete physical inventory and reconciliation; staff and resource constraints resulted in ongoing compliance only partial to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit an explanation on the noted overpayment equivalent to the market value of the 200 sq. m. discrepancy, and erroneous computation; and b. concerned Municipal personnel to institute appropriate action to recover the amount overpaid from the payee. Otherwise, persons responsible shall be held accountable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management informed that the amount of overpayment Ᵽ233,800.00 was returned by the payee; reference Official Receipt No. 30535101 dated May 31, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require: a. Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to expedite the production of lacking documents necessary to transfer title of the acquired land in the name of the Municipality; b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to submit updated report considering impact of Taal eruption; and c. Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to comply with documentary requirements supporting all future procurement, particularly land.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Assessor provided documents and tax payments; titling process is in progress. The Real Property Tax payment made and documents submitted to Office of General Services. The Municipal Planning and Development Officer to provide updated report. Some actions underway.
observations.reason: Titling process ongoing; some documentation submitted but transfer not yet completed; further validation and completion required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct: a. concerned officials to submit written justification why the payment requested was included and approved for 2020 prior to receipt thereof; b. MENRO and Municipal Accountant to submit Record for the Accomplishment Report/Service Record for the hauling activities; c. BAC and concerned officials to ensure future contracts include required particulars and documents as per COA Circular No. 2012-001; d. MENRO to adopt controls including trip tickets and records of attendance/service for contractor personnel; e. Municipal Accountant to use appropriate account (Environment/Sanitary Services) for garbage hauling/disposal expenses.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO reasoned that the supplier requested payment near year end; MENRO and Municipal Accountant to provide reports and trip tickets; new hauling contract in CY 2021 lacked particulars; actions ongoing to strengthen controls and documentation.
observations.reason: New hauling contract still lacked full compliance; documentation and controls being improved; trip tickets and contractor records not consistently available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. Municipal Accountant to adhere to proper accounting treatment of advances to contractors and recoupment; b. Municipal Engineer to strictly enforce posting of warranty security and ensure preliminary inspection and punch list; c. Municipal Engineer to conduct preliminary inspection at 95% accomplishment and prepare punch list; d. BAC to ensure all required documents attached and properly accomplished; e. discontinue practice of processing payments lacking sufficient documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and BAC to submit explanations and lacking documents. Some corrective actions and documentation procurement are ongoing. The Municipal Accountant to adjust entries where necessary.
observations.reason: Ongoing provision of supporting documents; some deficiencies remain and require remedial action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC, MGSO and MDRRMO to: a. submit the lacking supporting documents and valid explanation for non-compliance; b. strictly abide by basic controls on receipt, recording, distribution and reporting of donations be it cash or in-kind.
observations.managementAction: Management is amenable to recommendations and committed to submit lacking documents and follow required reporting procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat, MGSO and Municipal Accountant to submit valid explanation and supporting documents regarding deficiencies; ensure subsequent procurement supported with complete documentation and properly accomplished and signed documents; concerned departments to submit proof of distribution/utilization; BAC Secretariat to ensure compliance with posting requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Management concurred and committed to submit remaining documents not attached to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: Some documents remain to be submitted and procurement processes strengthened; actions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to provisions of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs whereby: a. creation of special account through maintenance of subsidiary ledger and preparation of appropriate reportorial requirements; and b. refrain from recording procured inventory items as outright expense; instead, recognize Inventory accounts and record issuance by Requisition and Issue Slip.
observations.managementAction: Management indicated compliance: they created special accounts and recorded inventories accordingly; supplies purchased were recognized and delivery/issuance procedures to be followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct: a. Municipal Treasurer to refund/return unexpended balance of Ᵽ13,000.00 to DSWD; b. MSWDO and Municipal Accountant to submit lacking documents such as original SAC forms, ID copies, photos, Certificate of Payment and proof of submission; c. MSWDO to coordinate with DSWD-Regional Office for confirmation of receipt; d. ensure payrolls indicate signature or thumb mark and date of receipt of payees; e. concerned officials to investigate the denial of receipt.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer refunded the unutilized balance of Ᵽ13,000.00 to DSWD with reference. The MSWDO coordinated and confirmed receipts; they will reconcile records and submit missing documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require: a. Municipal Planning and Development Council to assess viability of PPAs in planning stage; b. Municipal Development Council to conduct in-depth planning and prioritization to avoid realignment; c. MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure projects are covered by approved appropriation ordinance and AIP; d. MPDC and Municipal Accountant to reconcile records on utilization of 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: MPDC explained issues on land ownership delayed implementation of purchase of lot for Pabahay Phase III; MPDC and Municipal Accountant concurred and will reconcile records. Some projects implemented; reconciliation and documentation ongoing.
observations.reason: Delayed implementation due to ownership issues and incomplete reconciliation between offices; actions underway to reconcile and complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require: a. LDRRMC and LDC to assess viability of identified PPAs in planning stage; b. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to prepare and submit annual LDRRMF Investment Plan using prescribed COA format including projects charged to continuing appropriations and prior years’ unexpended funds; c. MDRRMO to submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to COA by 15th day after month-end; d. concerned officials to comply with RA No. 10121, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO explained she submitted quarterly reports and was not aware of monthly submission requirement; prospectively will comply with monthly submissions.
observations.reason: Monthly reporting not yet complied with; LDRRMFIP not prepared previously; utilization of funds not optimal; actions planned to comply prospectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and adjust accordingly based on determined disparities; b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers, intensify tax collection campaigns, issue demand letters and strengthen collection enforcement.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records has not been fully undertaken; demand letters and collection campaigns being planned; partial actions undertaken but full reconciliation not yet completed.
observations.reason: No full reconciliation completed as of year-end; some demand/collection activities yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate establishment and implementation of the programmed MRF and potable water projects costing Ᵽ475,000.00 and Ᵽ9,757,000.00 respectively to allow early enjoyment of benefits by the public.
observations.managementAction: Projects costing Ᵽ9,757,000.00 and Ᵽ475,000.00 were completed, inspected and accepted by the Municipal Engineer and Punong Barangays in CY 2021. Some transfers and reporting on unexpended balances being addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that end-users implement PPMPs; conduct market research to justify ABC; BAC to ensure complete bidding documents; BAC to ensure ABCs based on careful price determination; ensure enforcement of 1% retention on non-expendable goods or require bank guarantee.
observations.managementAction: No PPMPs were prepared by end-users; the BAC and concerned end-users were advised to prepare and comply with requirements; compliance ongoing.
observations.reason: Some requirements and corrective actions remain outstanding; compliance being pursued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. ensure availability of trained personnel to operate equipment and provide capability building; b. develop contingency plans for power failure and natural disasters and secure cold chain for reagents; c. establish mechanisms to protect and secure equipment within RHU compound including access control and security.
observations.managementAction: The Blood Chemistry Analyzer is being operated by a Licensed Medical Technologist who received training; for other equipment, trainings and procurement of necessary security and contingency have been prioritized but some constraints on progress due to COVID-19 response and resource limitations.
observations.reason: Training and securing of equipment partially completed; other measures pending due to resource and prioritization constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. stop practice of collecting laboratory fees without authority and submit justification; b. require concerned officials to submit written justification on unauthorized disbursements and ensure future expenditures adhere to approved appropriation/ordinance; c. design and implement process workflow for RHU operations and internal control to safeguard funds and properties.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Health Officer admitted lapses and confirmed enactment of Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance No. 07-2020 allowing laboratory fees; municipal officers to coordinate and submit documentation; some unauthorized disbursements covered by approved appropriations after ordinance; process workflows being developed.
observations.reason: Some corrective measures taken including SB ordinance; other internal control improvements and documentation still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices to report complete details of land account including Title No., location and land area and produce proof of ownership; verify and support transfers; and ensure compliance with documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted report containing parcels of land owned with details; some parcels reflected without complete title and ongoing titling and validation; actions being undertaken.
observations.reason: Some documents and titles remain to be obtained; titling and validation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer’s Office prepare road networks property cards and Report on Local Road Network; Municipal Engineer to provide complete description and cost segregation; Inventory Committee/Treasurer’s Office to conduct inventory count and submit RPC for road networks; Municipal Accountant to prepare property ledger cards and disclose in notes.
observations.managementAction: Treasurer’s Office in process of preparing road networks property cards; Municipal Engineer submitted revised report showing additional kilometers accounted; Inventory and reconciliation ongoing.
observations.reason: Work in progress; property cards and full physical count not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended revert long outstanding obligations to government equity with supporting documentation; send demand letters and document all actions taken; consider write-off only after diligent verification and with Sanggunian approval.
observations.managementAction: Municipal offices submitted requests for write-off and documentation for some accounts; some requests returned due to insufficient support. Further verification and documentation being pursued.
observations.reason: Requests for write-off returned due to inadequate supporting documents; further verification required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended continuous coordination with Provincial Accounting Office until accounts reconciled; where claims are documented, settlements should be made; if undocumented, consider write-off with Sanggunian approval.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant informed reconciliation with Provincial personnel is being undertaken; partial responses received; reconciliation ongoing.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with the Province is ongoing; some documentation missing and further coordination required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer assess probability of collection of delinquencies, enforce penalties and cancellations as warranted, and submit reports of uncollected rental fees each year-end to Municipal Accountant for recording and disclosure.
observations.managementAction: Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer informed Audit Team that almost 90 percent of delinquencies were collected; actions to enforce settlement and cancellations undertaken; however reporting to Municipal Accountant at year-end was not fully furnished.
observations.reason: Collections improved but reporting and formalized enforcement documentation incomplete at time of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended stoppage of paying/granting TA to personnel who are assigned or use government transportation and require refund of allowance paid; ensure compliance with regulations.
observations.managementAction: The concerned official refunded ₱2,000.00 as partial settlement via ORs 25425069 and 24432358 (₱1,000.00 each). Further refunds to be pursued.
observations.reason: Only partial refund made; full recovery pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the LCPC to prepare and submit required reports to assess implementation of child-related programs and ensure functionality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer submitted LCPC Accomplishment Report for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended activation of Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees, intensify dissemination and enforcement of No Segregation No Collection rule, instruct barangays to establish and operate their MRFs and consider clustered MRFs; Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate for closure and rehabilitation of existing MRF.
observations.managementAction: MSWMB reorganized through Executive Order No. 007A s.2020; dissemination campaigns and meetings conducted; some barangays implemented segregation and MRF activities; appropriation for MRF closure/rehab in CY 2020 was reversed. Ongoing efforts to intensify compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE annually and submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year;

The inventory taking shall be per PPE account/each category of assets and prepare the RPCPPE on the result thereof in conformity with the above-mentioned Manual for LGUs.

The accounting office and designated General Services Officer religiously maintain the PPELC and Stock Cards/Property Cards; and

The Accounting office and General Services Office reconcile PPE accounts between PPELC and Property Cards.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the correct depreciation expenses of above-mentioned assets and a journal entry must be drawn to update/correct the accumulated depreciation;

Exert effort through the General Services Office, to reconstruct and update its property cards and reconcile with accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation for those depreciable assets not provided with depreciation; and

Create an appraisal committee to conduct evaluation of assets whose records could not be found in order to establish the commencement of depreciation and determine the depreciable cost and estimated remaining life for the computation of the respective depreciation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of procurement;
 
Direct the responsible offices to submit to the Office of the Auditor the required documents for further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to always review the completeness of the supporting documents before the payment is being made.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to strictly comply with the mandatory posting requirements of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 to promote transparency and accountability for all emergency procurements relative in the implementation of Bayanihan Act. Regularly visit the GPPB’s website to be aware of any updates with their issuances to avoid any more non-compliance in the future.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Exert prudence in the disbursement of LDRRMF, see to it that only expenditures appropriately chargeable against the said funds are those in pursuant to the provisions of Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01 and Section 4.3 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular;

Submit satisfactory justification/legal basis to support disbursements of the PDRRM funds amounting to P362,166.00; and

Require the persons responsible for the overpayment of Job Order payrolls for the periods June 16-30, 2020 and November 16-30, 2020 to refund the amount of P3,000.00; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to cautiously check the completeness of all the pertinent supporting documents such as Accomplishment Reports for job order payrolls before payment is made.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to cautiously check the completeness of all the pertinent supporting documents before allowing the payment. Also, the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer request the Municipal Engineer to prepare program of works or program design/proposal of the Clean-up drive before the implementation of the project.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to regularly prepare the required Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the same will also be submitted regularly by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer within the prescribed period as pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly comply with the requirements of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the submission of the required list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year to facilitate regular monitoring and validation of the Municipality’s program/projects/activities.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of these Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of these Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant the following:
a) Properly provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
 
b)	Comply with all the conditions and requirements for possible filing for request of authority to write off dormant receivable accounts by the Local Chief Executive to the Commission on Audit.

To keep the accounts from inaccurate information, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to consistently verify and review all
the transactions and accounts balances before closing and preparing the year-end
financial statements; and
b. Municipal Accountant to incorporate in the notes to financial statements the
necessary information pertaining to the Municipality’s financial position and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
consult with the service provider the disparity between the consolidated SL balances
of all the accounts comprising the Cash in Bank, Local Currency Current Account and
its GL ending balances generated by ECPAC FMIS as well as other issues/concerns
pertinent thereto for immediately diagnosis and appropriate action.

undertake periodic reconciliation of the general ledger to sub-ledger balances of

Head of the Procuring Entity and other elected officials for purposes other than
official travelling expense. Ensure that no additional cash advance shall be granted to
permanent appointed officials or designated disbursing officers with outstanding
cash advances.

In reiteration of prior year’s recommendation we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer,
Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Engineer to exert utmost efforts in the

 Unauthorized disbursements amounting to ₱258,900.00
Article VI, Section 29 (1) of the 1987 Philippine Constitution and Section 4 of Presidential
Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 provides that no money shall be paid out of any public treasury except
in pursuance of an appropriation law or other specific authority.
Further, Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445 provides the following:
“(5) Disbursements or disposition of government funds or property shall invariably bear
the approval of the proper officials.
(6) Claims against government funds shall be supported with complete
documentation.”
Also, Section 85 (1) of P.D. No. 1445 states that “No contract … shall be entered into unless
there is an appropriation therefor, the unexpended balance of which, free of other obligations,
is sufficient to cover the proposed expenditure.”
In addition, Section 86 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 40, Chapter 5, Book VI of Executive
Order (E.O.) No. 292 provides that the Chief Accountant shall issue a Certificate of Availability
of Funds (CAF). The CAF shall be attached to and become an integral part of the proposed
contract, and “the sum so certified shall not thereafter be available for expenditure for any
other purpose until the obligation of the government agency concerned under the contract is
fully extinguished.”
Section 10 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations (IRR) provide that, as a general rule, “all procurement shall be done through
Competitive Bidding.” As an exception, Section 48-54 provided for the alternative methods of
procurement and the respective compliance requirements.
Further, pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR)
of R.A. No. 9184 are quoted as follows:
“7.1 All procurement shall be within the approved budget of the Procuring Entity
and shall be meticulously and judiciously planned by the Procuring Entity.
...
7.2 No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the
approved APP, including approved changes thereto. The APP must be
consistent with the duly approved yearly budget of the Procuring Entity and
shall bear the approval of the [Head of Procuring Entity (HoPE)] or second-
ranking official designated by the HoPE to act on his behalf. ...
7.3.2 In the preparation of the indicative APP, the end-user or implementing units
of the Procuring Entity shall formulate their respective Project Procurement
Management Plans (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities and
projects (PAPs). ...
7.3.4 The PPMPs shall then be submitted to the Procuring Entity’s Budget Office
for evaluation in order to ensure consistency with the Procuring Entity’s
budget proposal and compliance with existing budgeting rules. The PPMPs
included in the budget proposal shall be forwarded to the [Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC)] Secretariat for consolidation in an indicative APP, and to
the BAC for final recommendation of the appropriate procurement
modality.”
In line with this, the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Circular No. 08-2015
dated July 16, 2015 states that “[t]he APP shall contain the following information, among
others:
1. Name of the project/procurement;
2. Project management office/end-user unit;
3. General description of the project/procurement;
4. Procurement methods to be adopted;
5. Time schedule for a) advertisement/posting; b) submission and
receipt/opening of bids; c) award of contract; d) contract signing;
6. Source of funds; and
7. Approved Budget for the Contract.”
Meanwhile, Section 37 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs
provides that “Disbursements shall be covered by Disbursement Vouchers (DV) or payrolls…”
In addition, COA Circular No. 2012-001 enumerates the documentary requirements for all
types of disbursement of funds.
Audit disclosed that the Municipality failed to prepare the Project Procurement Management
Plan (PPMP) in relation to the operation and maintenance of the medical equipment funded
under the Grant Assistance for Grassroots Human Security Projects (GGP), and consequently
failed to incorporate such data in its Annual Procurement Plan (APP).
As a result, the disbursements made by the RHU totaling ₱258,900.00 was not covered by
an appropriation ordinance enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan, approved by the Municipal
Mayor and reviewed by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan. Hence, a forecast/estimate of the
supply requirements of the RHU for the medical equipment was not prepared, and
consequently, the Municipality failed to enforce measures to ensure that such requirements
will be met.
Examination of the logbook maintained by the Head Midwife and passbook for the bank
account of the Rural Health Unit (RHU) showed that expenses incurred by the RHU totaling
₱258,900.00, as detailed below, were charged to the funds held in trust for the operations of
the RHU, despite the lack of an approved appropriation ordinance authorizing such
disbursements.
